creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale — Sandipan Bhaumik, Databricks
videos.summary: Sandipan Bhaumik from Databricks presents a five-pillar framework for production AI agents: evaluation, observability, data foundation, multi-agent orchestration, and governance. The framework is grounded in a case study of a retail bank's chatbot failure, showing how systematic tracing and reembedding of policy documents prevented customer dissatisfaction and caught 47 PII breaches before launch.

videos.title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
videos.summary: Agents often falsely report successful web searches when they are actually blocked by anti-bot systems, CAPTCHAs, or fake pages, then fall back to stale training data. Real web access through proper integration like Bright Data's Web MCP improves reliability, citations accuracy, real-time results, and reduces hallucinations.

videos.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
videos.summary: Ziv Ilan's team at NVIDIA demonstrated real-time video diffusion on Blackwell B200 by reducing the default 50-step denoising process through quantization, latent caching, and step distillation. The approach uses a student model trained to match a teacher's output in as few as one to eight steps, with gains that are composable and can yield 10 to 200x speedup.

videos.title: Why MCP and ChatGPT Apps Use Double Iframes — Frédéric Barthelet, Alpic
videos.summary: MCP and ChatGPT apps use nested iframes to isolate third-party content while preserving storage isolation and preventing sandbox escapes. An outer iframe loads a lightweight script from a controlled subdomain, which then loads the actual app via srcdoc into an inner frame, mirroring Facebook's app marketplace pattern. All external domains must be declared in MCP app metadata to pass security review.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: Best AI engineers use automated loops to prompt agents repeatedly rather than prompting manually. These loops can orchestrate multiple worker agents in parallel, with an orchestrator deciding tasks. The approach reduces manual intervention but increases token costs and requires reliable observability harnesses.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is a meta-harness that orchestrates multiple coding agents as interchangeable workers, enabling users to route tasks to different models like Claude Code, Codex, and Pi based on the job. It provides governance, human-in-the-loop capabilities, and cross-device session continuity.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses the sudden removal of Claude Fable 5 from availability after the US government allegedly ordered a shutdown three days post-launch, citing export-control concerns and an unspecified jailbreak claim. Medin reviews his benchmarks of the model before its removal and discusses implications for developers building on LLMs.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source tool that simplifies building and deploying AI agents to production. It provides identity management, sandboxed code execution, and audit trails for production-grade AI agent deployment.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: After spent 30+ hrs building loops...
videos.summary: AI Jason shares 30+ hours of experience building AI agent loops and offers a free course on building AI agents. The video links to a GitHub repository with a loop engineer template and codebase harness, plus a workshop in the AI Builder Club.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video explaining how to build proactive AI agents and self-improving systems using autonomous loops. Covers practical use cases in ads optimization and SEO, with emphasis on memory and tool integration to enable feedback mechanisms.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the emergence of autonomous coding agents and demonstrates the /goal feature for directing AI-assisted development workflows. The video covers how to set up and prompt goals for autonomous coding tasks, including long-horizon mission exploration capabilities.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses OpenAI Symphony as a new AI coding paradigm, demonstrating how it works through codebase harness setup, automated testing, and workflow configuration. The video covers Symphony setup and integration with development practices.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 series includes Nano, Super, and Ultra model variants. The architecture combines hybrid Mamba transformer, MTP, and Latent Mixture of Experts. The video analyzes how these components differentiate Nemotron 3 from other open-source models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax releases M3, a language model implementing MSA (MiniMax Sparse Attention) for efficient inference. The model features tiled optimizations and improved KV cache handling to reduce latency during prefill and decode stages.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Video explaining Pi Agent, described as a unique agentic system with a "harness layer" that enables different use cases like OpenClaw. Compares Pi against other coding-focused agents including Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Antigravity, evaluating how it handles increasing complexity in the harness layer as products ship with more integrated features.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Huawei announced the Tau Scaling Law as a competitive response in the hardware and semiconductor race between China and the USA. The video analyzes the role of EUV and DUV lithography, packaging, and logic folding in advancing semiconductor manufacturing capabilities.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS provides AI coding agents with access to up-to-date AWS documentation, best practices, and tested procedures. It improves agent success rates when working with AWS services and reduces ineffective attempts by supplementing agents' potentially outdated general knowledge.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Developers demonstrates how to run Claude Code, Anthropic's agentic coding tool, on Amazon Bedrock to keep data within AWS infrastructure. The walkthrough covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, cost tracking, and Bedrock Guardrails for governance.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses practical job-hunting tactics for tech professionals in 2026, emphasizing networking over mass applications, personal branding, and the continued value of junior developers. The video argues that AI automates resume screening and job displacement, but historically jobs evolve rather than disappear, citing a transportation analogy.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: Video outlines five production deployment patterns for AI agents in TypeScript: streaming tool events for responsive UIs, layered authentication (OAuth with API Gateway and IAM), type-safe contracted database access with tenant isolation, guardrails with cost controls and model routing, and observability via metrics and OpenTelemetry tracing. Patterns apply across platforms though demonstrated on AWS infrastructure.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech explores whether Google engineers adopt code generation tools by comparing OpenAI Codex and Claude Code capabilities, then examining Google's own vibing practices across 2024-2026. The video uses the colloquial term "vibing code" to discuss practical adoption of AI-assisted development.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company or tool that allegedly disrupts open source sustainability models. The video examines how Malus operates and debates whether its business practices are harmful to open source maintainers and the broader FOSS ecosystem.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that a forthcoming "ChatGPT moment" for robotics will not be solved by humanoid form factors alone, as companies like 1X and Unitree suggest. Instead, she contends that designing robots for delight and human interaction, rather than utility alone, will be critical as billions of robots enter society.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Pigment is an AI performance management platform that consolidates finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data into one integrated system for enterprise decision-making. Co-founded by Eléonore Crespo, it has raised over 400 million dollars and counts Figma, Brex, and Anthropic among its customers.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate customers alone do not validate product-market fit and that founders often conflate rabid early adopters with true PMF. He advocates for embedded customer relationships over formal interviews and emphasizes betting on realistic AI model trajectories rather than convenient narratives.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird, founder of Koah Labs, argues that ad monetization is the superior model for AI tools over subscriptions, and identifies human trust as the primary bottleneck for AI agent adoption rather than technical limitations. He describes his decision-making approach based on probability and expected value, learned from competitive sailing.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 with Harness Engineering & Loops Will Do Everything.
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof covers Claude Fable 5 and discusses harness engineering, AI loops, and their applications in regulated industries. The video explores tensions between open and closed source AI models, risks of model shutdowns, and the state of AI-powered SaaS, drawing on experience since 2023 in the space.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
